title: Correct resolution scaling without shrinking layout
overview: The renderer currently calls setSize(w, h) which sets both the drawing buffer AND inline CSS dimensions, then immediately overwrites CSS back to 100%. This belt-and-suspenders approach is fragile on mobile. The fix is to pass updateStyle=false to setSize() and let CSS exclusively own the canvas layout, while setPixelRatio() handles quality scaling.

changes: Change renderer.setSize(w, h) to renderer.setSize(w, h, false) at line 44 and line 69
Remove manual style.width/style.height assignments at lines 45-46 and 70-71 (CSS handles this)
Change renderer.setSize(width, height) to renderer.setSize(width, height, false) at line 23
Remove manual style.width/style.height assignments at lines 24-25 (CSS handles this)
Change renderer.setSize(w, h) to renderer.setSize(w, h, false) at line 88
Remove manual style.width/style.height assignments at lines 89-90 (CSS handles this)
Verify canvas CSS already has width: 100% !important and height: 100% !important (already present, no change needed)

files_to_create.description: Unit tests verifying: (1) renderer.setSize is called with updateStyle=false, (2) setPixelRatio is called with capped DPR * resolutionScale, (3) camera aspect updates on resize, (4) orientationchange triggers handler
Unit tests verifying: (1) initScene calls setSize with updateStyle=false, (2) no inline style.width/height set on canvas by initScene, (3) context restore re-applies pixel ratio without touching CSS

details: In initScene(): change renderer.setSize(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight) to renderer.setSize(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight, false)
Remove the two lines: renderer.domElement.style.width = '100%' and renderer.domElement.style.height = '100%' after setSize
In onContextRestored(): same changes — setSize with false, remove manual CSS lines
In onResize(): change renderer.setSize(width, height) to renderer.setSize(width, height, false)
Remove the two lines setting renderer.domElement.style.width/height to '100%'
In the geoPromise.then callback: change renderer.setSize(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight) to renderer.setSize(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight, false)
Remove the two lines setting renderer.domElement.style.width/height to '100%'
Test that attachResizeHandler calls setSize with (width, height, false)
Test that setPixelRatio is called with Math.min(dpr, 2) * resolutionScale
Test that camera.aspect and camera.updateProjectionMatrix are called
Test that orientationchange event triggers the resize handler
Test that visualViewport resize triggers the handler

steps.action: Write tests for scene initialization scaling
details: Test that initScene calls setSize with updateStyle=false (third arg)
Test that renderer.domElement has no inline width/height style after init
Test that context restore applies pixel ratio correctly without CSS manipulation
Test that resolutionScale option is applied to setPixelRatio
Run npm run build to confirm no type errors
Run test suite to confirm all tests pass
Verify: renderer DOM element CSS is always 100% of container (from stylesheet, never from JS)
Verify: quality scaling only touches setPixelRatio, never canvas CSS dimensions
Verify: no black bars scenario — canvas CSS is fixed at 100%, drawing buffer scales independently

acceptance_verification.renderer_dom_matches_viewport: CSS rule canvas { width: 100% !important; height: 100% !important } ensures DOM element always fills viewport. JS never sets inline CSS dimensions.
acceptance_verification.quality_scales_pixel_ratio_not_dom: All setSize calls use updateStyle=false. Quality tier only affects setPixelRatio(Math.min(dpr, 2) * resolutionScale). Canvas CSS dimensions are never touched by quality logic.
acceptance_verification.no_black_bars: Canvas CSS is always 100% of #app container which is position:fixed covering full viewport. setSize(w, h, false) never interferes with this layout.
acceptance_verification.orientation_changes_handled: attachResizeHandler listens to resize, orientationchange, and visualViewport resize events. All three recalculate viewport size, update camera aspect, and call setSize with false.

risks.risk: Three.js internal viewport calculation may differ when updateStyle=false
risks.mitigation: setSize still sets internal _width/_height correctly regardless of updateStyle flag — verified in Three.js source
